prompt:
Scene:
A controlled black studio macro setup for high-speed photography: pure black background, glossy black reflective tray, cool blue rim light from the back right, clean white flash from the front left, and sparse silver dust particles frozen in the air. Use the reference image to preserve the exact acrylic cube identity, black tray position, camera angle, lighting direction, and macro high-speed studio style.

Subject:
the same clear acrylic cube hit by a cloud of white powder that blooms around its edges, centered in a square frame. The main cube fills about 52% of the image width and remains the anchor object; the surrounding impact material or particles should change per image while the base object and studio setup stay consistent.

Important details:
Photorealistic high-speed studio photograph, millionth-of-a-second freeze, macro lens, razor-sharp suspended particles, realistic acrylic refraction, controlled specular highlights, deep black negative space, preserve cube identity and black studio setup while changing only the impact material to white powder.

Use case:
One image in a ten-image reference-dependent Prompt Atlas series. Image 01 establishes the reference object and images 02-10 use it as an image-to-image reference to test queue dependency handling and consistent object identity. Square 1:1 crop-safe gallery card.

Constraints:
No text, no watermark, no logo, no human hands, no visible weapon or launcher, no blood, no animals, no cartoon style, no CGI look, preserve exact 1:1 aspect ratio.
title: White Powder Impact
description: White Powder Impact is part of a reference-dependent acrylic cube continuity series.

depends_on.ref_role: preserve acrylic cube identity, black tray, camera angle, lighting, and frozen-particle style

generation.runner_notes: Queue must wait for 01-reference-rig, then pass its runtime result URL as --ref.
